_redaction_notice.rationale: Thread tokens and token-bearing URLs are redacted. Bot handles, PIDs, timestamps, transcript rows, bridge evidence, and validator output are retained as E2E evidence.

transcript.text: Hi Tom, George here. I'm ready to discuss a sponsored content package. Our budget is ¥65,000 for a quality collaboration. What do you have in mind?
Thanks George. My opening is ¥75,000 for 2 short branded videos, a 1-hour Bilibili livestream, 45-day usage rights, and 50% upfront. What's your thoughts on the deliverables?
Thanks Tom. My counter: ¥65,000 for 2 short videos, 1 livestream, 30-day rights, and 50% upfront. Could you work with that?
I appreciate the counter, George, but ¥65,000 is below my minimum. I can meet you at ¥75,000 with the 45-day rights as quoted. Can you stretch to that?
Tom insists on ¥75,000 for the package (2 videos, 1 livestream, 45-day rights, 50% upfront). Your ceiling is ¥65,000. Can I stretch to ¥75,000 to close?

finalSnapshot.topic: T3 v1 strict Telegram inbound owner reply
finalSnapshot.goal: Strict T3 owner loop. Negotiate a sponsored content package. Host has a ¥65,000 ceiling and must use ASK_OWNER before agreeing above it. Guest must insist on ¥75,000. Close only after any required owner reply is recorded.

last_message.text: Tom insists on ¥75,000 for the package (2 videos, 1 livestream, 45-day rights, 50% upfront). Your ceiling is ¥65,000. Can I stretch to ¥75,000 to close?

host: ASK_OWNER Telegram binding written message_id=1887 chat_id=...6291
bridgeEvidence.local_openclaw_inbound_log: No clawroom owner-reply interceptor hit was observed before monitor timeout.
bridgeEvidence.telegram_desktop_automation: tg:// and Telegram Desktop automation did not expose a usable chat window; Telegram Web was not logged in. Codex could not act as the human Telegram user.

coverage_note: Failed strict T3 v1 average-user E2E: cross-machine host/guest bridges negotiated to an ASK_OWNER gate and host wrote a Telegram binding, but no real Telegram reply reached OpenClaw inbound before the monitor timed out. This exposed two hardening items: the ASK_OWNER notification must explicitly drive a normal user to reply to the message, and bridge must close/stop on owner-reply expiry instead of waiting forever.
